title: ubuntu 22.04 missing zlibc
body:
Without `zlibc` in ubuntu 22.04 is it safe to install Verilator without it? The instructions say `sudo apt-get install zlibc zlib1g zlib1g-dev  # Ubuntu only (ignore if gives error) `. I assume if not available ignore installing it?

We are on a 22.04 image for other reasons and would love to keep Verilator on the same image so everyone can use the same image regardless of engineering discipline. 

I had to use `autoconf-archive` instead of `autoconf` on 22.04 to install SystemC. From trying it the 22.04 autoconf works fine for building verilator.

In case it helps others, we built the latest SystemC and discovered that the archive was required to resolve an issue with `AC_PROG_LIBTOOL` not being found. So I ended up doing `apt purge -y autoconf && apt-get -y install autoconf-archive libtool libltdl-dev` and then after pulling down SystemC inside the systemc directory `autoupdate && autoreconf -i` before configure and make.

**TL;DR**
A co-worker was chasing some odd behavior with our SystemC model using 2.3.3. We were using clang 12, tried g++, moved to ubuntu 22.04 from 20.04, tried clang 14 but still was seeing odd behavior exiting a user function. Ended up building SystemC from [4a44d36](https://github.com/accellera-official/systemc/commit/4a44d3619eca48a7a99122ae8c0fe3baf101d2e5) after seeing valgrind errors on SystemC 2.3.3. This is the latest currently and it is also valgrind clean.
